sutta: the buddha said monks that a disrespectful and unruly monk not dwelling in harmony with his fellows in the holy life will keep the minor precepts such a thing is not possible that he will keep the learner's dharmas without keeping the minor precepts is not possible that he will keep the aggregate of moral conduct without the learner's dhamma is not possible that he will keep the aggregate of concentration without moral conduct is not possible that he will attain the aggregate of wisdom without concentration such a thing is not possible but monks that a respectful and controlled monk dwelling in harmony with his fellows in the holy life will keep the minor precepts such a thing is possible that he keeping the learners keeping the minor precepts will keep the learners dharmas such a thing is possible and that he keeping the learner's dharmas will keep the moral conduct such a thing is possible that he keeping the moral conduct will attain concentration such a thing is possible and he attaining concentration will attain wisdom such a thing is possible that's the end of the suta
commentary:
so here the buddha is trying to say that firstly a monk has to be respectful and control and dwelling in harmony with his fellows in the holy life such a man will be 1 who is sincere about the holy path who's sincere about practicing the spiritual path such a person it is possible that he is willing to cultivate the minor precepts and when he cultivates the minus precepts he will progress to keep the learner's dharma the learner's code of things that must be practiced and then if he does that then he will progress to attain or keep the moral conduct and when he keeps the whole aggregate of moral condom then he will attain concentration and when he attains concentration he will attain wisdom such a thing is possible but the reverse is not possible that means if he is then he will not be able to keep the minor precepts and such a person will not be able to keep the learner's dharmas will feel to keep the moral condom and because of that you will fail to attain concentration and a person who fails to attain concentration the buddha said now it is impossible that he will attain wisdom you see here that the final partner that without attaining concentration a person cannot get wisdom and if you look into other sutas you'll find the reason is that the buddha said without abandoning the hindrances we cannot get wisdom and the way to abandon the hindrances is to attain concentration another thing i'd like to know about this uttar is that this type of among the disrespectful and unruly monk not in harmony with his companions in the holy life he's a type of monk which has no hiri or tapa earlier i think you heard me talking about the learner's powers he talked about hiri or tapa here is a term which means conscience all conscientiousness means a man who is uh has a sense of shame he's afraid to do wrong and o tapas a sense of remorse if among does something wrong he feels remorse there are some people who don't feel remorse for the wrong that they have done and so such a manga with a sense of shame and then only he can progress but among without a sense of shame not fearing to do evil and after doing evil not remorseful he cannot progress in the holy life and also you see in this uh the few steps here in the sutta that it is a gradual path the holy life is a gradual path no step is to miss to be missed out now first a monk will be sincere he got to be respectful harmonious monk then he will progress to keeping the minor precepts and they will progress to keeping the learners dharma and from there the whole aggregate of moral condom and from there to concentration to wisdom this gradual training that the buddha expects in the holy life
